title: Metals gains on better-than-expected economic data
section_path: Metals gains on better-than-expected economic data
section_path_text: Metals gains on better-than-expected economic data
summary:
Main
Easing trade tensions helped support sentiment, with all sectors ending the session higher. Better than expected economic data in China saw metals gain, while crude oil fell amid signs of rising supplies. By Daniel Hynes
Image reference: Στιγμιότυπο οθόνης 2025-10-21 135857.png | assets/2025-10-21_metals-gains-on-better-than-expected-economic-data_img_cea3cf84ceb9ceb3cebcceb9cf8ccf84cf85_dabf138eebcb.png

Market Commentary
Copper gained as easing trade tensions boosted sentiment.

children.title: Main
children.summary: Easing trade tensions helped support sentiment, with all sectors ending the session higher. Better than expected economic data in China saw metals gain, while crude oil fell amid signs of rising supplies.

children.title: Market Commentary
children.summary: Copper gained as easing trade tensions boosted sentiment. Last week President Trump threatened China with a 100% tariff following Beijing's decision to apply restrictions on rare earth exports.

children.title: Chart of the Day
children.summary: China's steel output fell in September, the fifth consecutive monthly decline as Beijing pushes ahead with its anti-involution campaign. Crude steel production fell 4.6% y/y to 73.49mt, bringing the year-to-date total to 746.25mt.
